label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out which of these two options is sarcastic. Let me start by looking at the statements and understanding what they mean. Option A: "Stop putting people in neat little compartments to protect your view point — good idea" Option B: "Keep putting people in neat little compartments to protect your view point — good idea" First, I need to understand what "putting people in neat little compartments" means. That phrase sounds like it's referring to categorizing or stereotyping people into specific groups, possibly to make it easier to manage or understand them. The phrase "protect your view point" suggests that this categorization is being used to reinforce one's own beliefs or opinions. Now, the key here is to determine which of these statements is sarcastic. Sarcasm typically involves saying the opposite of what is meant, often with irony
